outlook.headline: Memory supply stays tight, though shortage pressure is no longer accelerating
outlook.summary: Mentions surged earlier and are still above baseline, but the latest 0.6x reading and +14% vs trailing average suggest tightness is settling in rather than intensifying further. Supply remains constrained by AI pull, lead-time/bit-capacity bottlenecks, and thin availability, which favors suppliers and substitution/pricing names like Micron, Arrow, FormFactor, MaxLinear, and AOSL; by contrast, memory-heavy downstream players are still seeing shipment friction or weaker demand. Watch whether new capacity ramps or inventory build at distributors starts to loosen the market in 2026Q1.

drivers.label: AI demand is absorbing available memory supply
drivers.detail: Severe undersupply and AI-driven pull are keeping supply short, with capacity expansion limited by how fast supply can come on.

drivers.label: Bit growth remains constrained by manufacturing bottlenecks
drivers.detail: Cleanroom, lead-time and mix constraints are still limiting DRAM/NAND bit growth and keeping availability tight.

drivers.label: Thin supply is supporting pricing and substitution
drivers.detail: Customers are substituting around shortages and suppliers are retaining leverage on pricing/value proposition.

drivers.label: Near-term capacity additions are beginning to ease the shortage
drivers.detail: Some reads point to new memory capacity and higher inventory, suggesting the squeeze is less acute than earlier.

drivers.label: Downstream demand is still being hit by memory constraints
drivers.detail: Shortages are constraining notebooks, PCs and other customer shipments, which can mute visible demand even when supply is tight.

risks: Faster-than-expected DRAM/NAND capacity ramps or yield gains could loosen supply sooner than expected.
Distributor/OEM inventory normalization could reduce urgency and soften the tight-supply signal.
Weaker end-demand in PC, mobile or consumer devices could curb pull-through even if supply stays constrained.
